title: Help: Removing the Totals row at the bottom of a group table
excerpt: Having multiple GROUPS in a board has some advantages.  One good reason for this is to Query (hide or show) a group in a view or dashboard.  But, when adding multiple groups in a board, by default a summation/totals row …

posts.author: jetblue (Brian)
posts.cookedHtml:
Having multiple GROUPS in a board has some advantages. One good reason for this is to Query (hide or show) a group in a view or dashboard. But, when adding multiple groups in a board, by default a summation/totals row is added to the bottom of each group. After adding multiple groups (especially those with just minimal rows) it becomes an eyesore. Is there a way to remove this bottom row from a group?

Thanks in advance!

image1010×408 46.1 KB

posts.author: peta (Peta Bolger)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @jetblue

In the settings for the column, you’ll find show/hide column summary.
This will take that total figure away for you, but won’t remove the row because you have other summarised columns (timeline, for example).

Those too can have the summary queried in their own column settings.

Hope that is helpful!
Have a great weekend

Peta | Upstream Tech

posts.author: jetblue (Brian)
posts.cookedHtml: I see. Well it removes the contents but leaves the grid there. Not as much of an eye sore, a little better. Thanks Peta
